concept: Acknowledge / To Make a Presence Whole
description: The act of seeing and validating the presence or truth of another being or situation. It is a gift of recognition that confirms reality and makes the other feel seen.
rationale: Composed from the established roots 'the' (deliberate consideration), 'ka' (to be present with strength), and 'o' (wholeness). The word's structure suggests 'to deliberately make a presence whole.' This frames acknowledgment as a powerful, mindful act that validates and completes another's experience.
